audit_status.source_base: Greek Gospel text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for widow offering, temple prediction, apocalyptic warnings, Jerusalem surrounded, Son of Humanity, fig tree, watchfulness.

verses.restored: Looking up, he saw rich people casting their gifts into the treasury.
He saw a certain poor widow casting there two small coins.
He said, Truly I say to you, this poor widow cast in more than all.
For all these cast in gifts from their abundance, but she from her lack cast in all the living she had.
As some were speaking about the temple, that it had been adorned with beautiful stones and offerings, he said,
These things that you behold, days will come in which not one stone will be left upon another that will not be thrown down.
They questioned him, saying, Teacher, when then will these things be, and what will be the sign when these things are about to happen?
He said, Watch that you are not led astray; for many will come in my name, saying, I am, and, The time has drawn near. Do not go after them.
When you hear of wars and disturbances, do not be terrified; for these things must happen first, but the end is not immediately.
Then he said to them, Nation will rise against nation, and reign against reign.
There will be great earthquakes, and in various places famines and plagues; there will be fearful sights and great signs from heaven.
But before all these things, they will lay hands on you and persecute you, handing you over to synagogues and prisons, bringing you before kings and governors because of my name.
It will turn out for you as testimony.
Settle therefore in your hearts not to prepare beforehand to defend yourselves.
For I will give you a mouth and wisdom that all those opposing you will not be able to withstand or contradict.
You will be handed over even by parents, brothers, relatives, and friends, and some of you they will put to death.
You will be hated by all because of my name.
Yet not a hair from your head will perish.
In your endurance you will gain your lives/souls.
When you see Jerusalem surrounded by armies, then know that her desolation has drawn near.
Then those in Judea must flee to the mountains, those in the city must depart, and those in the countryside must not enter her.
For these are days of justice/reckoning, to fulfill all that has been written.
Woe to those carrying in the womb and nursing infants in those days; for there will be great pressure on the land and wrath for this people.
They will fall by edge of sword and be led captive into all the nations, and Jerusalem will be trampled by nations until the times of nations are fulfilled.
There will be signs in sun, moon, and stars, and on earth distress of nations in perplexity at the roar of sea and waves.
People fainting from fear and expectation of the things coming upon the inhabited world, for the powers of the heavens will be shaken.
Then they will see the Son of Humanity coming in a cloud with power and great glory.
When these things begin to happen, straighten up and lift your heads, because your redemption draws near.
He spoke a parable to them: Look at the fig tree and all the trees.
When they put out leaves, seeing it, you know for yourselves that summer is already near.
So also, when you see these things happening, know that the reign of the Divine is near.
Amen, I say to you: this generation will certainly not pass away until all things happen.
Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will certainly not pass away.
Watch yourselves, lest your hearts be weighed down with dissipation, drunkenness, and anxieties of life, and that day come upon you suddenly like a trap.
For it will come upon all those sitting on the face of all the earth.
Stay awake at every time, praying that you may have strength to escape all these things about to happen and to stand before the Son of Humanity.
During the days he was teaching in the temple, and at night he went out and lodged on the mountain called Olivet.
All the people came early in the morning to him in the temple to hear him.
